user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: clean scientific visualization with realistic aircraft wing and semi-transparent airflow lines
style_bible.camera_rules: smooth cinematic motion only when specified; explanation moments must be completely static
style_bible.lighting_rules: neutral daylight with soft highlights emphasizing airflow and wing curvature
style_bible.continuity_rules: aircraft wing orientation, airflow direction, and camera side must remain consistent across shots and clips

clips.clip_script: Introduce the airplane wing in flight and visualize how air splits and accelerates differently above and below the wing.

shots.framing: wide side view of airplane wing against sky
shots.subject_action: airplane wing glides steadily as airflow lines approach the leading edge
shots.continuity_note: airflow lines approach wing and begin to divide, leading into closer examination

shots.subject_action: airflow lines clearly split above and below the wing, with faster motion on top
shots.continuity_note: viewer understands speed difference as airflow continues moving past wing

shots.framing: angled close-up following airflow over wing
shots.subject_action: airflow streams stretch and accelerate over the wing surface
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: close-up of wing top surface with fast-moving airflow frozen as reference for clip 2 start

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

clips.clip_script: Build from the same wing view to explain pressure differences and how they generate lift.

shots.framing: matching close-up of wing top surface
shots.subject_action: airflow resumes from previous frozen frame, continuing smoothly
shots.continuity_note: direct continuation from clip 1 final frame with identical orientation

shots.framing: cutaway view showing pressure zones around wing
shots.subject_action: colored pressure shading appears, lower pressure above wing, higher below
shots.continuity_note: pressure zones establish cause of lift as airflow continues

shots.framing: slightly wider view showing upward force arrow
shots.subject_action: a subtle upward arrow illustrates lift acting on the wing
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: wing held steady with visible upward lift force implied

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

full_script_20s: A realistic airplane wing in flight is shown. Airflow lines reveal how air moves faster over the curved top of the wing than beneath it. A static hold highlights pressure differences. The view continues seamlessly as airflow speed increases, demonstrating how lower pressure above the wing creates lift, ending on a clear upward force visualization.
